FluentGpu
Table of Contents

Class ReactiveComponent

Namespace
FluentGpu.Hooks
Assembly
FluentGpu.Hooks.dll

A signals-native component: Setup() runs ONCE at mount (the SolidJS model) — create signals, wire bindings (TransformBind/For/Show) and return the tree. The component never re-renders; fine-grained bindings update exactly the nodes that read a changed signal. Use this for the highest-performance UI; use plain Component when you prefer the re-render model (still granular).

public abstract class ReactiveComponent : Component

MotionHooks.UseEntrance(Component, float, object?)
MotionHooks.UseHoverScale(Component, bool, float)

Properties

RunsOnce

True for run-once (signals-native) components: the reconciler runs their body untracked, so the render-effect never re-subscribes and never re-renders — reactivity comes purely from the bindings/For/Show inside. False for classic Components, whose render-effect re-runs on their own state/context changes (granular).

public override sealed bool RunsOnce { get; }

Property Value

bool

Methods

Render() 

public override sealed Element Render()

Returns

Element

Setup()

Build the tree once. Reads of signals here do NOT subscribe a re-render — bind them instead.

public abstract Element Setup()

Returns

Element
FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
